name: Yi Sun-sin
description: Yi Sun-sin (1545-1598) was a Korean naval commander renowned for his victories against the Japanese navy during the Imjin War (1592-1598). Born into a noble family, Yi Sun-sin excelled in military arts and was appointed to a naval post. Recognizing the deficiencies of the Korean navy, he implemented innovative shipbuilding techniques and developed tactical strategies that exploited the geography of the Korean coastline. His most famous innovation was the Geobukseon, or Turtle Ship, an armored warship that proved devastating against Japanese fleets. Throughout the war, Yi Sun-sin won numerous key battles, including the Battle of Hansan Island and the Battle of Myeongnyang, where he famously defeated a much larger Japanese fleet with only thirteen ships. His strategic brilliance, unwavering courage, and commitment to his nation made him a national hero in Korea. Even after facing political intrigue and temporary demotion, he returned to command and continued to defend Korea. He tragically died in the final battle of the war at Noryang Point, but his legacy as a brilliant military leader and a symbol of Korean patriotism endures. His diaries and writings provide valuable insights into 16th-century naval warfare and his unwavering dedication to his country.
subtitle: Korea's Invincible Admiral
achievements: Won the Battle of Hansan Island
Successfully defended Korea against Japanese invasion
companions: Various unnamed loyal officers and sailors

keyMyths.title: The Thirteen Ships
keyMyths.description: The Battle of Myeongnyang is legendary because Yi Sun-sin, with only thirteen ships, decisively defeated a vastly superior Japanese fleet. This victory saved Korea from further invasion and showcased Yi Sun-sin's tactical genius.
keyMyths.source: Nanjung Ilgi (War Diary of Yi Sun-sin)

keyMyths.title: The Turtle Ship
keyMyths.description: The Geobukseon, or Turtle Ship, was an innovative armored warship that was instrumental in Korean victories. Its iron-clad design and powerful cannons made it nearly invulnerable to Japanese attacks.
keyMyths.source: Various historical records and naval texts

keyMyths.title: Yi Sun-sin's Loyalty
keyMyths.description: Despite facing false accusations and demotion, Yi Sun-sin remained loyal to his country. He accepted his punishment without complaint and returned to service when his nation needed him most.
keyMyths.source: Nanjung Ilgi (War Diary of Yi Sun-sin)

sources.author: Yi Sun-sin
sources.description: Yi Sun-sin's personal war diary, offering detailed accounts of battles, strategies, and his personal reflections.
sources.text: Yi Sun-sin's personal war diary, offering detailed accounts of battles, strategies, and his personal reflections.

sources.description: A comprehensive historical account of the Imjin War, providing context and analysis of Yi Sun-sin's role.
